title: Intensely Peppery Russian Beef Dumplings
description: These fluffy Russian dumplings are a crowd-pleaser, filled with a peppery beef mixture and steamed to perfection in a bamboo steamer, featuring muscovado sugar and grilled to add a smoky flavor, making them an anti-inflammatory friendly dish.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, black pepper, muscovado sugar, onion, garlic, and salt, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Gradually add the flour to the beef mixture, mixing until a dough forms.
Add the egg to the dough and mix until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and shiny.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, and place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat the yakimono-grill over medium-high heat, and brush the dumplings with vegetable oil.
Grill the dumplings for 2 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown.
Place the dumplings in a bamboo steamer, and steam for 10 minutes, until they are cooked through.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
